examples: Let go of the door handle, please!
relinquish your grip on the rope--you won't fall

meanings.definition: grant freedom to; free from confinement
let (something) fall or spill from a container
examples: turn the flour onto a plate

meanings.definition: prepare and issue for public distribution or sale

examples: combustion products are exhausted in the engine

meanings.definition: generate and separate from cells or bodily fluids
examples: release a hormone into the blood stream
release the list with the names of the prisoners

meanings.definition: part with a possession or right
examples: I am relinquishing my bedroom to the long-term house guest
resign a claim to the throne

meanings.definition: release (gas or energy) as a result of a chemical reaction or physical decomposition

examples: release the holdings in the dictator's bank account
